evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my React Native app. Here's my screen file: EmptyApp.tsx. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyApp.tsx as a function component with hooks (DataCaptureView + BarcodeCaptureOverlay + Camera), and shows the setup checklist (packages to install, npx pod-install, camera permission entries).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-react-native-datacapture-core and scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions npx pod-install for iOS
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription in ios Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions Android runtime camera permission (PermissionsAndroid) or notes Android auto-links
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
DataCaptureContext.sharedInstance is used to obtain the context
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
A license key placeholder string is present (semantic)
new BarcodeCapture(settings) is present (v8 constructor used, not BarcodeCapture.forContext)
dataCaptureContext.addMode( is called to attach the BarcodeCapture mode to the context
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies except EAN13UPCA and Code128 are enabled
<DataCaptureView is rendered as JSX with a context prop
BarcodeCaptureOverlay is constructed and added to the DataCaptureView via addOverlay or withBarcodeCaptureForView (semantic)
Camera.default is present
dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource(camera) is called
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called to start the camera
The component is written as a function component (no extends React.Component / class syntax)
useRef is used to hold the BarcodeCapture mode reference across renders
addListener is called on the BarcodeCapture mode to register a listener with a didScan callback
The didScan callback sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false (or mode.isEnabled = false) before processing the scan

Imports come from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core (DataCaptureContext, DataCaptureView, Camera, FrameSourceState) and scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode (BarcodeCapture, BarcodeCaptureSettings, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, Symbology)

evals.prompt: Add BarcodeCapture barcode scanning to ProductListApp.tsx. When a barcode is scanned, append it to the scannedProducts state and re-render the product list. Keep the existing clear button and list rendering.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeCapture to ProductListApp.tsx. The existing scannedProducts state, render logic, and clear button are preserved. Scanned barcodes are appended via setScannedProducts.

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
new BarcodeCapture(settings) is present
dataCaptureContext.addMode( is called
<DataCaptureView is rendered in the component's JSX
BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created and added to the DataCaptureView
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called with a didScan callback
didScan callback pushes / appends to scannedProducts via setScannedProducts (or equivalent state setter)
Existing scannedProducts state and setScannedProducts setter are preserved
Existing list rendering (FlatList / ScrollView / map) is preserved
Existing clear button / onClearTapped handler is preserved
useEffect cleanup calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...)

evals.prompt: I want to draw a square viewfinder over the camera preview in my BarcodeCapture React Native app so users know where to aim.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property.

assertions.text: new RectangularViewfinder( is constructed
RectangularViewfinder is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
The viewfinder is assigned to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay (overlay.viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved

evals.prompt: Customize the success feedback in my BarcodeCapture React Native app so it vibrates but does NOT play a sound on each scan.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a Feedback to barcodeCapture.feedback.success with a default vibration and a null sound, using BarcodeCaptureFeedback.defaultFeedback as the starting point.

assertions.text: BarcodeCaptureFeedback.defaultFeedback (or new BarcodeCaptureFeedback()) is used as the starting feedback
feedback.success is assigned a new Feedback instance
Vibration.defaultVibration is passed to the Feedback constructor
null is passed for the sound argument of Feedback (no sound)
barcodeCapture.feedback is set to the customized feedback
Imports for Feedback and Vibration are added from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
Imports for BarcodeCaptureFeedback are added from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved

evals.prompt: I only want to accept barcodes that appear inside a circular region in the middle of the frame (50% of the smaller dimension). Restrict the scan area in my BarcodeCapture React Native app.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.locationSelection to a RadiusLocationSelection with a 0.5 fraction radius and applies the settings to BarcodeCapture.

assertions.text: new RadiusLocationSelection( is constructed
NumberWithUnit and MeasureUnit.Fraction are used to express the radius
settings.locationSelection is assigned the RadiusLocationSelection
Settings are applied to the BarcodeCapture mode either via barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings) or by passing settings into the constructor before the mode is added to the context (semantic)
RadiusLocationSelection, NumberWithUnit, MeasureUnit are imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to filter scans so duplicates within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance before constructing the BarcodeCapture mode (or applies updated settings).

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter = 500 is set (or equivalent in the API units)
codeDuplicateFilter is set before constructing BarcodeCapture, or barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings) is called after the change (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved

evals.prompt: Switch my BarcodeCapture React Native app to manual scan intention so the SDK does not try to guess which barcode the user wants.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.scanIntention = ScanIntention.Manual and applies the settings.

assertions.text: settings.scanIntention = ScanIntention.Manual is set
ScanIntention is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-core

The updated settings (including scanIntention) are applied to the BarcodeCapture mode, either by calling barcodeCapture.applySettings(settings) or by passing the configured settings into the BarcodeCapture constructor (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when my React Native scan screen unmounts — stop the camera, remove the overlay, and unbind the BarcodeCapture mode from the context so its native resources are released.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires the cleanup function in useEffect to call switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off), removeOverlay, removeListener, and dataCaptureContext.removeMode(barcodeCaptureRef.current).

assertions.text: useEffect cleanup (returned function from useEffect) calls dataCaptureContext.removeMode(...)
The BarcodeCapture mode passed to removeMode is the one held in the useRef
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) (or useFocusEffect cleanup) stops the camera
dataCaptureView.removeOverlay(...) is called for the overlay (in the cleanup or via the useEffect return)
dataCaptureContext.dispose() is never actually invoked in the cleanup logic — it may appear in a comment explaining the rule, but no executable call to dispose() exists (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved

evals.prompt: Change the symbologies my BarcodeCapture React Native app accepts to only QR and DataMatrix.
evals.expected_output: The skill replaces the enabled symbologies with Symbology.QR and Symbology.DataMatrix and re-applies the settings.

assertions.text: Symbology.QR is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
The active enabled symbology list no longer includes Symbology.EAN13UPCA (it may still appear in a removed/before comment, but is not in the call to enableSymbologies) (semantic)
The active enabled symbology list no longer includes Symbology.Code128 (it may still appear in a removed/before comment, but is not in the call to enableSymbologies) (semantic)
settings.enableSymbologies(...) is called with QR and DataMatrix
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to enable composite codes (CompositeType A) alongside the regular barcodes in my BarcodeCapture React Native app.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.enabledCompositeTypes to include CompositeType.A and calls settings.enableSymbologiesForCompositeTypes(...) to enable the symbologies the composite type needs.

assertions.text: settings.enabledCompositeTypes is assigned an array containing CompositeType.A
settings.enableSymbologiesForCompositeTypes(...) is called with the composite types
CompositeType is imported from scandit-react-native-datacapture-barcode
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeCapture mode, DataCaptureView JSX, listener, overlay) is preserved
